faqs.q: How much does tree removal cost in Lake Charles, LA?
faqs.a: Tree removal in Lake Charles typically ranges from $350 to $2,200 depending on tree size and location. Storm-damaged trees often run $500–$1,000. We offer free on-site estimates and work with homeowners insurance.

faqs.q: Do you offer emergency tree service after storms?
faqs.a: Yes — Lake Charles sees significant hurricane and tropical storm activity. We provide 24/7 emergency response for storm-damaged trees, fallen trees on structures, and urgent hazard removal throughout Calcasieu Parish.

faqs.q: Will insurance cover tree removal after a storm in Louisiana?
faqs.a: Louisiana homeowners insurance typically covers removal when a tree falls on and damages a covered structure. Removal of storm-toppled trees that didn't cause structural damage may not be covered. We provide documentation for insurance claims.

faqs.q: Do you serve Sulphur, Westlake, and Moss Bluff?
faqs.a: Yes — we serve all of Calcasieu Parish including Sulphur, Westlake, Moss Bluff, Iowa, Vinton, DeQuincy, and surrounding communities.

faqs.q: How quickly can you respond after a storm?
faqs.a: We prioritize storm response and typically have crews dispatched within hours of major weather events. Call immediately after any storm damage — we triage by severity.
